description_long: This is a two-story stone house with a flat, textured roof and a partially collapsed facade, exposing the interior structure and debris. The front features arched doorways and rectangular windows, some of which are missing or broken due to the damage. The stone exterior is composed of rough, gray blocks, and the upper story has a row of shuttered windows, giving it a traditional, utilitarian appearance. The right side of the building is heavily damaged, with large sections of wall and floor missing, revealing broken floors and rubble spilling out. The rear and left sides are more intact, with symmetrical window arrangements and a continuous stone texture. The overall look suggests an older, possibly abandoned residential building that has suffered significant structural failure.
description: A damaged two-story stone house with a flat roof, arched doorways, and symmetrical shuttered windows, featuring a large collapsed section exposing the interior.
description_view_0: Front view showing arched doorways, symmetrical windows, and a large collapsed section with rubble spilling out.
description_view_1: Right side view highlighting the extensive wall and floor collapse, with debris scattered outside.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying intact symmetrical rows of shuttered windows and an undamaged stone facade.
description_view_3: Left side view with a mostly intact stone wall, minor roof damage visible at the top corner.
attribute_entryway_design: Arched main entryway with no visible door, no porch or columns, and a simple, utilitarian design.
